title: sigstore-keyless signing
manager: Certificates get identities — sigstore-style keyless signing bound to who ran the check (a CI workflow identity or a maintainer's OIDC identity), so a certificate says not just "this was checked" but "this was checked by this identity." Keyless means no long-lived signing keys to leak; the identity comes from an OIDC provider at signing time, which is how modern supply-chain signing sidesteps the key-management disaster entirely.
cs: sigstore-style OIDC keyless (CI workflow / maintainer identities); `fsl login` local vs CI-OIDC flows; no long-lived keys; the identity layer over M2 signing (W8.19).
